Experience
Database Administrator III Full-time
BNY Mellon International Operations (Egypt) Jan 2020 - Present
•Installed and configured database servers using slipstream, setting up all maintenance jobs as per standard policy
on standalone and cluster environments with latest service packs.
•Installed SSRS, uploaded .rdls, and assigned correct data sources to reports, granting necessary access to users &
developers on reporting website; gained awareness of SSIS package design.
•Created and managed logins and users for database applications, assigning permissions as per requests, and resolv-
ing user login issues.
•Migrated all SQL Server 2005/2008 servers to higher versions, ensuring seamless transition and minimal downtime.
•Set up database refresh jobs on QA, DEV , and UAT environments, efficiently fixing orphaned users.
•Troubleshot complex performance-related issues, optimizing database operations for enhanced efficiency.
•Contributed to multiple projects, collaborating with developers to provide essential support for testing in QA, UAT
& DEV environments.
•Led Disaster Recovery (DR) tests for the database team, ensuring robust recovery plans and system resilience.
•Participated in database purge and archive activities, maintaining data integrity and storage efficiency.
•Developed and implemented automation scripts for various database administration tasks, improving operational
efficiency.
•Automated DR tasks including starting agent jobs on multiple servers and restoring databases for log-shipped envi-
ronments without manual intervention post-DR.
•Provided comprehensive support to vendor databases, managed vendor calls, and timely escalated issues to ensure
quick resolution.
•Installed and configured smsql on Windows Server, scheduled jobs for creation and deletion of clones on SQL Server,
and maintained backups.
•Installed and configured MongoDB server, creating databases and collections as per project requirements.
•Created new users and granted access using MongoDB Ops Manager, ensuring secure database access.
•Monitored MongoDB database servers effectively using Ops Manager to ensure optimal performance and health.
•Managed and maintained multiple instances of Oracle & Sybase Databases on Linux and Windows servers.
•Monitored daily jobs, including backups, refreshes, and maintenance tasks for various database systems.
Database Administrator Full-time
HP Egypt (on payroll of Softenger Solutions) May 2017 - Dec 2019
•Installed SQL Server on standalone and Windows cluster environments wit

h latest service packs, including slipstream
installations.
•Installed and configured reporting services (SSRS) for business intelligence solutions.
•Created logins and users, assigning appropriate permissions as per access requests, maintaining security.
•Conducted security audits for all logins, including active management of unused and orphaned user logins.
•Created & maintained daily and weekly jobs/maintenance plans, encompassing backup, index rebuild/reorganization,
update statistics, and database consistency checks.
•Established and maintained linked servers, ensuring reliable connectivity between diverse server environments.
•Proactively monitored disk space and managed storage efficiently using data and log file shrinking techniques.
•Monitored blocking, deadlocks, open transactions, and slow-running queries during performance issues, providing
critical insights to developers.
•Configured alerts for deadlock and blocking scenarios to proactively maintain and improve database performance.

•Implemented and troubleshot high availability technologies such as Log Shipping, AlwaysOn, and Mirroring, with
knowledge of Replication.
•Successfully completed database migrations from one server to another, ensuring data integrity and system stability.
•Performed Disaster Recovery (DR) drills (Online/Offline) on a quarterly basis to validate recovery procedures.
•Utilized PowerShell scripting to monitor and restart SQL services, and to set up email alerts for service status.
•Maintained TNS entries for Oracle client configurations as per client requirements, ensuring connectivity.
•Interacted with customers to gather requirements and provided timely updates on the status of handling issues and
service requests.
•Managed proper escalation and notification matrix for all support levels, ensuring effective issue resolution.
Projects
Certificates
Skills
Operating Systems: Microsoft Window Server 2003 - 2012
Database Technologies: SQL Server - Sybase ASE Server - Oracle - MongoDB
Monitoring & Ticketing: HP Service Manager 7.0/9.0 - SolarWinds DPA - JIRA - MongoDB Ops Manager
Web Server: IIS 7.0
Database Tools: SSMS - DBArtisan - Studio 3T - SnapShot Manager for SQL Server
High Availability: Log Shipping - AlwaysOn - Mirroring - Replication
Scripting: PowerShell

Drills - Security Audit - User Management - Query Optimization
Extracurricular Activities

Ali Khalil is a Database Administrator with contact information including email (ali.khalil@email.com), phone number (+20 100 123 4567), and links to his GitHub (github.com/ali-khalil) and LinkedIn (linkedin.com/in/ali-khalil) profiles, as well as a personal website (alikhalil.com).
Ali Khalil received a B.Tech in Computer Science from PDACOE, Gulbarga, an autonomous institution in Gulbarga, Karnataka, India.

From January 2020 to the present, Ali Khalil worked as a Database Administrator III at BNY Mellon International Operations (Egypt), where he installed and configured database servers, managed SSRS and SSIS, created and managed database logins, migrated SQL Server versions, set up database refresh jobs, and troubleshooted performance issues.

At BNY Mellon, Ali Khalil also led Disaster Recovery (DR) tests, participated in database purge and archive activities, developed automation scripts, automated DR tasks, provided support to vendor databases, and installed and configured smsql and MongoDB.

Ali Khalil created databases and collections in MongoDB, managed user access using MongoDB Ops Manager, and monitored MongoDB servers for performance and health. He also managed Oracle & Sybase Databases on Linux and Windows servers, and monitored daily jobs like backups and refreshes.
From May 2017 to December 2019, Ali Khalil worked as a Database Administrator at HP Egypt (on payroll of Softenger Solutions) and installed SQL Server on standalone and Windows cluster environments.

The individual installed and configured reporting services (SSRS) for business intelligence solutions, created and managed user logins with appropriate permissions, and conducted security audits to maintain database security.
Daily and weekly jobs/maintenance plans were created and maintained, including backups, index rebuilds, statistics updates, and database consistency checks.
Linked servers were established and maintained to ensure reliable connectivity between different server environments, and disk space was proactively monitored and managed using shrinking techniques.
Performance issues were monitored for blocking, deadlocks, open transactions, and slow queries, providing insights to developers, and alerts were configured for deadlock and blocking scenarios.
High availability technologies like Log Shipping, AlwaysOn, and Mirroring were implemented and troubleshot, with knowledge of Replication.
Database migrations were successfully completed from one server to another, ensuring data integrity and system stability.
Disaster Recovery (DR) drills were performed quarterly to validate recovery procedures.
PowerShell scripting was utilized to monitor and restart SQL services and set up email alerts for service status.
TNS entries were maintained for Oracle client configurations to ensure connectivity, and customer requirements were gathered with timely updates provided.
A proper escalation and notification matrix was managed for all support levels to ensure effective issue resolution.
The individual possesses skills in installation, configuration, performance tuning, migration, backup & recovery, DR drills, security audit, user management, and query optimization.

Ali Khalil is a highly capable and proactive Database Administrator, a digital architect ensuring the smooth operation and integrity of critical data systems. Educated with a B.Tech in Computer Science from PDACOE, Gulbarga, he possesses a solid foundation in the field, consistently applying and expanding his expertise over the past several years. Currently at BNY Mellon International Operations (Egypt) since January 2020, he’s a key player responsible for the full lifecycle of database management – from installation and configuration of SQL Server, SMSQL, and MongoDB, to meticulous performance monitoring and troubleshooting. 

He’s adept at navigating complex database environments, managing SSRS and SSIS, and expertly handling user access and security. Ali doesn’t simply maintain systems; he optimizes them, automating disaster recovery tasks and developing scripts to streamline operations. His experience extends to leading crucial Disaster Recovery tests, managing database purges and archives, and providing support for vendor databases. Prior to BNY Mellon, he honed his skills at HP Egypt (through Softenger Solutions) from May 2017 to December 2019, gaining experience installing SQL Server in both standalone and clustered environments. 

Beyond SQL Server, Ali demonstrates versatility with MongoDB, skillfully creating databases and collections, and leveraging MongoDB Ops Manager for access control. He’s equally comfortable managing Oracle and Sybase databases across both Linux and Windows platforms, ensuring the reliability of daily backups and refreshes. A resourceful problem-solver and a dedicated professional, Ali Khalil can be reached at ali.khalil@email.com, +20 100 123 4567, or through his online presence at github.com/ali-khalil, linkedin.com/in/ali-khalil, and alikhalil.com.

Windows Server is a robust and versatile platform, a foundational element in the architecture of complex data environments. It functions as a reliable host for critical database systems – Oracle, Sybase, and SQL Server – providing the operating environment for their operation, particularly within clustered configurations as demonstrated by installations at HP Egypt. More than just a host, it’s a key component in ensuring business continuity; at BNY Mellon, it underpins disaster recovery strategies, supporting the smooth execution of automated DR tasks. Increasingly, it’s also embracing NoSQL solutions, providing the infrastructure for MongoDB deployments, where Ali Khalil expertly managed database creation, user access via Ops Manager, and ongoing performance monitoring. It’s a workhorse, quietly supporting both traditional relational databases and modern data stores, and a crucial element in maintaining data integrity through automated backups, refreshes, and archive processes. It’s a platform built for stability, scalability, and the demanding needs of enterprise-level data management.
